economic_hypothesis: A transition in which completed ES event clock is faster, volume or dollar, reveals a change in the size/notional composition of aggressive participation; participants extrapolating the prior composition can pay either delayed continuation or inventory reversal after the transition.
who_pays: Liquidity suppliers and late aggressive followers whose inventory and execution response remains calibrated to the previously faster event clock.
persistence_rationale: The comparison is ordinal and threshold-free after the frozen construction of each event clock; it measures a transition in trade-size composition rather than the same-minute flow agreement tested by grammar 0004.

source.past_only_pairing: At each completed event availability timestamp, use that event and only the latest already completed event of the other clock in the same explicit contract and session.

motifs.definition: volume duration becomes strictly shorter than the latest completed dollar duration after previously being greater than or equal
dollar duration becomes strictly shorter than the latest completed volume duration after previously being greater than or equal
either speed leadership transition occurs while the two latest completed bars have nonzero opposite signed-aggressor-flow signs

signal_policy.decision_timestamp: availability_ns of the event that causes the frozen speed-leadership transition
signal_policy.entry: first explicit-contract 1m open at or after decision timestamp
signal_policy.exit: explicit-contract 1m open at entry plus frozen holding_minutes
signal_policy.motif_direction: signed-aggressor-flow sign of the newly faster completed event clock

CONTRE: Both clocks are constructed from the same prints and their duration thresholds are frozen but related; the transition may still be generic activity geometry rather than a tradable economic mechanism.
